title: chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. simpliciusculum
intro: Chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. simpliciusculum, also known as the yellow buttons or yellow daisy bush, is a flowering plant native to Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its antiseptic and expectorant properties, offering benefits such as wound healing and relief from congestion. Herbal preparations of Chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. simpliciusculum include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken orally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
